fields.description:
## Why

The additive-attestations rollout (epic dark-acme-lumen) kept a legacy single-slot
`state['signature']` mirror alongside the authoritative kind-keyed `state['attestations']`
map — the *expand* half of an expand/contract. This ticket ships the *contract* half: stop
carrying the mirror in new SNAPSHOTs. Dropping it is a one-way door (a pre-attestations clone
reads `state['signature']` and has no map to fall back to), so it is gated on a readiness
condition, made fully reversible by config, and documented.

## What (ordered so no reader ever sees a stripped mirror)

1. **ADD a NEW helper** `signing.most_recent_attestation(state)` (does not exist today; this
   ticket creates it). Semantics: return the record with the greatest `signed_at` from
   `state['attestations']` (ties → last processed, matching the legacy mirror's
   last-writer-wins); fall back to `state['signature']` only when the map is absent/empty.
   This reproduces the mirror's "latest signature of any kind" from the authoritative map.
2. **Migrate all three in-tree mirror readers to call that helper** (so they no longer read
   `state['signature']` directly), done BEFORE step 3 so a stripped mirror is never observed:
   `signing._record_for_kind` (kind=None verify-latest path), `_engine_support/validate.py`
   (`signature_findings` — same "most-recent record, verify_record it" semantics, now
   map-sourced), and `_commands/txn.py:_signature_gate` (line 362, require_signature_for_close).
   Because `attestations` is always present on a post-feature snapshot (and old snapshots are
   folded into it on read), every migrated reader still finds the record after the drop.
3. **Only then drop the mirror on the write side:** `compact.py` strips the legacy `signature`
   key from a SNAPSHOT's compiled_state (normal-compaction AND fsck-rebuild paths), gated by a
   NEW `compact.emit_legacy_signature_mirror` config flag (default `false` = drop).
4. Keep the read-side fold-in (`_processors.py`) so pre-map snapshots still upgrade on read.
5. Document the readiness gate, rollback, and target version.

## Scope

`src/rebar/_config_schema.py`, `src/rebar/_commands/compact.py`, `src/rebar/signing.py`,
`src/rebar/_engine_support/validate.py`, `src/rebar/_commands/txn.py`, `docs/migrations.md`,
tests under `tests/unit/`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] [operator-attested] Readiness gate satisfied before the mirror is dropped: no
  pre-attestations clone/reconcile-host that reads `state['signature']` remains live. Evidence:
  this fleet auto-updates hourly to origin/main (so no pre-attestations binary is live), and
  the gate + upgrade-first ordering is documented in docs/migrations.md "Legacy
  signature-mirror retirement". Recorded as a ticket attestation.
- [ ] Rollback + named target version: the drop is reversible with NO code downgrade — setting
  `compact.emit_legacy_signature_mirror = true` (env `REBAR_COMPACT_EMIT_LEGACY_SIGNATURE_MIRROR`)
  re-emits the mirror on the next compaction. Named rollback target: that flag on the 0.7.x
  line (introduced after 0.7.1). Documented in docs/migrations.md; verified by a unit test that
  the rollback flag keeps `signature` in the snapshot.
- [ ] Contract: a new SNAPSHOT's compiled_state contains `attestations` but NOT the legacy
  `signature` mirror (default flag), and docs note the field retired. Verified by unit tests
  (`tests/unit/test_legacy_signature_mirror_drop.py`) covering the strip default, the
  consumers resolving via the map, a mirror-less snapshot round-trip, and old-snapshot
  fallback; docs/migrations.md documents the retirement.

## Verification

- `make lint && make typecheck` pass; `pytest tests/unit/test_legacy_signature_mirror_drop.py`
  and the signing/attestation suites (test_signing, test_attestations_reducer,
  test_attestation_verify, lifecycle/test_signature) pass with no regressions.
